observations.statement: The staged contract documents GET /api/v2/download/status with one required query parameter, file_name, taken from a prior download endpoint response.
The documented 200 response includes status, message, file_name, file_url, total_size, total_columns, total_rows, and seconds_elapsed.
The documentation enumerates status values failed, finished, ready, and running; it shows file_url as a relative path and says total_columns, total_rows, and total_size are null until finished.

source.title: Current raw MCP profile for download status

observations.statement: The current raw profile exposes file_name as the only request field and records prior 400 Missing one or more required query parameters and 404 Download job with filename ... does not exist error payloads that contain only detail.
The current raw profile's prior live probes observed both running and finished responses with absolute HTTPS file_url values rather than the relative path shown in docs.
The current raw profile recorded a running response with numeric total_columns and total_rows before completion, contradicting the documentation claim that they stay null until finished.
The current raw profile notes case-sensitive query parameter handling and last-value-wins behavior for repeated file_name parameters, but those edge cases were not re-probed in this bundle yet.

source.title: Download status view and shared download URL builder

observations.statement: The view reads request.query_params.get('file_name'); if the value is missing or blank it raises InvalidParameterException with the message Missing one or more required query parameters: file_name.
Successful responses echo job_status.name, error_message, file_name, get_file_path(file_name), total_size derived from file_size / 1000, total_columns, total_rows, and seconds_elapsed().
Outside local development, get_file_path(file_name) returns a storage URL from S3Handler rather than a relative /csv_downloads path.
The same DownloadStatusViewSet is mounted at both /api/v2/download/status and /api/v2/bulk_download/status.

source.title: Download job lookup table and elapsed-time model method

observations.statement: The download job lookup table defines status names ready, running, finished, failed, queued, resumed, created, and uploading.
seconds_elapsed returns a Python timedelta for running, finished, and failed jobs, which the API serializes as a string in existing observed responses.
number_of_rows and number_of_columns are stored independently from file_size, so source code does not require them to stay null until a job is finished.

source.title: Derived MCP request coverage check for download status

observations.statement: The current raw MCP profile already exposes the only material request field, file_name, so this semantic bundle does not add missingImportantRequestFields; its added value is contradiction handling, alias/workflow context, and error-shape semantics.

source.title: Live prerequisite probe to create a fresh download job

observations.statement: A bounded award download probe returned a fresh file_name and status_url that point at /api/v2/download/status, confirming the operational join from download initiation to this status endpoint.
The initiating response used an absolute status_url and file_url in production.

source.title: Live probe of download status with a fresh file_name

observations.statement: GET /api/v2/download/status returned HTTP 200 for a fresh file_name and reported status running, confirming live availability on 2026-05-12.
The live response returned message null, an absolute HTTPS file_url, total_size null, numeric total_columns and total_rows while the job was still running, and seconds_elapsed as a string.
